details.full_description:
A bright, spacious and creative home in Helsinki! 

My home has three bedrooms, one with a double bed, one with a bunk bed and one with a loft bed and a couch that can be turned into a single or a double bed. In the spacious living room there are 2 more futon sofa beds. All the bedrooms have also a table for working.

The apartment is well isolated and really quiet and warm even in the winter. The windows are on three sides of the apartment, so there is plenty of sun light from morning until evening.

The kitchen is fully equipped and a dining table for 6-7 people. Two fridges and freezers ensure that there is always enough space for your food.

The sunny, glassed balcony is perfect for having a breakfast or dinner in the evening. Because of the glassed walls it gets warm on sunny days even if it's just 5 degrees outside. 

The apartment has also a private sauna (fits 3 - 4 persons) to enjoy the true Finnish relaxation.
details.full_area_description:
The new apartment building is situated in the area of the old train factory, which is turning into a new cultural hot spot, so it's a nice combination of modern and historical Helsinki. 

The neighborhood is an old working-class district, which is now popular among creative people, but also families. The area is really safe for kids and there are several playgrounds and parks nearby. In the winter you can go sledging to a nearby park. In 2025 Timeout ranked the neighbourhood the 9th coolest in the world: https://www.timeout.com/travel/coolest-neighbourhoods-in-the-world-2025

There are a lot of restaurants and green space in a walking distance, and Kallio, the most hip area of Helsinki, can be reached in 10 min by foot.

In the summer you can walk to swim in a swimming stadion or take a bus (or bike) to some of the nice beaches of Helsinki. Helsinki has a big network for city bikes and there is a bike station next to the house. If you want to go for a walk or run, you can go the nearby bay that also leads to the center of Helsinki. You can also reach the central park (which is actually a forest) easily.

The area is peaceful, but well situated: the connections to all parts of the city are really good by trams, busses and trains.

There is a direct train connection between the airport and Pasila railway station (10 min by foot or one stop by bus). Also every train leaving or arriving Helsinki stops in Pasila, so you can easily travel to other parts of Finland by train. From the harbours you can reach the apartment by trams. 

There is a 24/7 grocery store (5 min walk) and 24/7 supermarket (at Pasila railway station) nearby.

details.full_area_description:
Punavuori: Helsinki’s Bohemian Design Hub

Nestled in the heart of Helsinki, Punavuori is a vibrant and eclectic neighborhood known for its artistic flair and rich history. Once a traditional working-class area, it has transformed into a trendy district popular among artists, designers, and creatives. ​

As part of Helsinki's renowned Design District, Punavuori boasts a plethora of unique boutiques, contemporary art galleries, and innovative restaurants. The streets are lined with colorful facades and cozy cafes, offering a dynamic blend of old-world charm and modern design. ​

The neighborhood's central location ensures easy access to the city's main attractions, while its proximity to parks and the sea provides a tranquil retreat from urban life. Whether you're exploring local markets, enjoying the vibrant nightlife, or simply strolling through its picturesque streets, Punavuori offers an authentic Helsinki experience.

The closest beach is just a 5-minute walk away for swimming in the sea. By the seaside, you will also find attractions such as Löyly, a café, restaurant and sauna experience. https://www.loylyhelsinki.fi/
details.full_other: The facade renovation will last the whole summer. Our balcony will be unaccessible during the renovation. The view from the windows of our livingroom, kitchen and master bedroom will be covered by scaffolding. The view from two smaller bedrooms overlooking the courtyard should not be obscured during the summer months. We do not have detailed schedules of the works at this stage, but there will be occasional noise and dust at regular weekday working hours. The windows facing the scaffolding will likely need to stay completely shut. Workers may also access the balcony from outside when working on the balcony surfaces - again we don’t know if and when, but want to give an accurate picture of the situation. All windows do have blinds so you can close them to ensure privacy, but daylight to the flat will be limited. As far as we know, the loudest phase will take place in April-May, perhaps early June.

details.full_area_description:
Helsinki, the capital of Finland, boasts a rich culture and an honest, relaxed vibe. With a population of less than 1.5 million residents, it’s also very approachable. Visit the city during summer to get a taste of the lifestyle, and enjoy its lovely nearby beaches and islands. 

The Kalasatama area has been an industrial and port area since the late 1800s. The port was moved to Vuosaari at the end of 2008. Kalasatama offers dense urban living close to services and public transport. The area includes excellent transport connections to several directions. The sea is present everywhere, and the Isoisänsilta Bridge provides convenient access to the Mustikkamaa recreational area and Korkeasaari Zoo. 

The wide selection of shops, restaurants and bars in the shopping mall Redi and Teurastamo area caters to the residents of Kalasatama. In addition to this, Suvilahti area serves as an event hub including e.g. famous Flow and Tuska festivals.

https://www.visitfinland.com/en/

https://www.myhelsinki.fi/en/see-and-do-neighbourhoods/kalasatama-kulosaari-and-mustikkamaa/kalasatama

details.full_other:

Within walking distance you’ll find grocery stores, restaurants, cafés, gyms, and entertainment. The Konepaja area itself features, among other things, a cinema, gym, indoor shooting range, indoor golf, padel courts, and several nice places to eat and spend time.

Ask for more details!
details.full_area_description:
We live in Punavuori district. Punavuori is a district in the southwestern part of downtown, right in the heart of Helsinki. It is known for its neighbourhood shops, design shops, art galleries, cafes, museums, restaurants, parks, the sea shore, little islands, community atmosphere and lively feeling. Public transportation is easily reachable (trains, metros, trams, buses, city bikes). 

Today some 9,000 residents call Punavuori their home. The streetscape is diverse with lot of art nouveau stone castles and neo-Renaissance houses. The district has also retained some of its old wooden housing blocks. Also the sea shore is very near, just about 1 km from our house. There are plenty of playgrounds also very nearby. 

Some call Punavuori "Brooklyn in Helsinki", and that might be true : ) The vibe is really good as there’s a nice mix of people from every profession. There’s a great sense of community here and everyone loves to support  their local. The atmosphere in general is relaxed but still unique.

The sea shore is 5 minutes walk away. You can easily manage your exchange even without the car, if you prefer using public transport.

The apartment is 100 m2 and has one double bedroom with ensuite bathroom and a second bedroom with pull out bed for two. We love contempo

We are only steps away from the main department store Stockman, railway station and Kamppi shopping centre and galleries. Our neighbouring park is an ancient cemetery where people nowadays enjoy their time on a sunny day. Twice a year there is a "Restaurant Day" where private people sell their home made food in the park and on other locations nearby. You have all the shops around you, cafes, b
listings.other_notes:
The most central location in Helsinki to live in!
Trendy restaurants just around the corner.
Enjoy our collection of modern art.

listings.title: Petra's urban studio with a yard
listings.description:
A compact home that has everything one would need. Ideal for 1-2 travellers. The building, built in 1912, used to house the factory workers of the nearby train factories, and nowadays all the apartments have been turned into studios with sleeping balconies/lofts (so maybe not for someone super tall or with bad knees as the the balcony has rather low ceiling!). The flat has been meticulously & beautifully renovated.

The flat is perfect for one, comfortable for two and can squeeze 3 if need be fo

The old train factory area nearby is being turned in to a Berlin-reminiscent culture hub. Everything you acutely need within walking distance (Pasila railway station, Tripla mall with 24h supermarket & alko, local grocery stores Lidl and Alepa very nearby, parks, pharmacy etc.) and city centre within reach by walking (30-45 minutes), biking (15 mins), tram (10-15 mins) or buses.

listings.area_description: Guests will love Sörnäinen for its unique blend of urban edge and cozy charm. It's super quiet here and you can't hear the airplanes at ALL, which we love as some light sleepers. There's one of Finland's biggest shopping centers a 15-minute walk away from us. It's called Jumbo, and as you can ima

Since we’re located in Jätkäsaari, one of Helsinki’s urban islands, sooner than later you will stumble upon the Baltic Sea in your strolls around this quaint neighborhood. You can even walk to the ferry terminal, hop on a ferry and visit Tallinn, why not?

The big windows brighten up the apartment from early morning throughout the day, and there’s a small balcony, perfect for morning coffee or a breath of fresh air. Notice that it is forbidden to smoke on the balcony. If you are visiting in the summer, there are window blinds and blackout curtains

There are two parks close by, which are lovely to hang out in during summer. In one of the parks, Sinberychoff Park, there are free pilates and yoga sessions organised each week. Public transportation is very accessible, but I often prefer to just walk.

Our home is spacious and quiet 4- bedroom apartment on the 7th floor in a lively neighborhood close to city center attractions. You can walk to town or take a metro, a tram or a bus. After a busy day of sightseeing in Helsinki you can relax here, warm up the sauna and have a cold drink in the balcony! Kids are more than welcome and they will have lots to do inside and outside play area.

Kallio is a lively neighborhood with cafes, bars, restaurants and boutiques. The amusement park Linnanmäki is a walk away and Korkeasaari Zoo, too.
The best way to start your day is to have porridge or coffee at Hakaniemi Market square.  Also from Kallio you access any part of Helsinki easily.
listings.other_notes:
City center within walking distance
Airport bus stop 5 minute walk. 
5 min walk to Sörnäinen subway.

Kallio is a lively neighborhood with cafes, bars, restaurants and boutiques. The amusement park Linnanmäki is a walk away and Korkeasaari Zoo, too.
The best way to start your day is to have porridge or coffee at Hakaniemi Market square.  Also from Kallio you access any part of Helsinki easily.
details.full_other:
City center within walking distance
Airport bus stop 5 minute walk. 
5 min walk to Sörnäinen subway.

There are also museums nearby, for example Finland's largest Design museum, and much more to admire. Right next to it is one of the city's most popular parks.

Situated on the 10th floor, atop a shopping mall and metro station, it offers unbeatable connections to both the bustling city center—just 10 minutes away by metro—and the serenity of nature. The neighborhood is called Kalasatama, or Fiskehamnen in Swedish (both Finnish and Swedish are national languages in Finland). The name reflects its history as a former harbor for fishing boats, and its location beautifully showcases this heritage—surrounded by the sea and connected to the city center by scenic bridges.

listings.title: Anna’s city apartment
listings.description: Smart city apartment, great for exploring Helsinki!

listings.title: Home in the heart of Helsinki
listings.description:
Our home is a beautiful space where we spend a lot of time. We live right in the heart of Helsinki: open kitchen and living room windows are onto a busy street, and bedroom windows open up to a private courtyard. The location is very central but still quiet to sleep and chill at home. In the building there is a beautiful rooftop terrace with barbecue, sunbeds and dining tables.

Our home provides everything you might need for a lovely stay in Helsinki: dishwasher, washing machine, microwave oven
listings.area_description: We live right in centre of Helsinki. It is possible to walk around the city easily from home, and there are also many public transportation options available very close by (tram, metro, bus, train). There's many grocery stores nearby; Lidl and two K-Markets are located just on the next street only minutes away. Our home is surrounded by lovely cafes, museums, galleries, restaurants, parks and shops. Staying at our place offers you a unique opportunity to experience Helsinki as a local.
listings.other_notes: The apartment is on the 4th floor without an elevator, so please do keep this in mind!

listings.title: Family home with balcony downtown Helsinki
listings.description:
Our home is wonderful for families or couples who like to wonder around Helsinki and Southern Finland. We are centrally located 10minutes tram ride to city center, 5 minute walk to forest and 10 minute walk to the beach.

We have lots of toys for the children, a couple of bikes with children's seats that can be borrowed if wanted. 

We also have a cabin at the lake Saimaa that we can consider for the exchange. You can see the pics from our listing.

5th floor, spacious flat.

The neighborhood is peaceful and the city center is just a walk, bicycle ride, or a couple of tram stops away.

Katajanokka is 8 minutes away via tram from the center of Helsinki, a garden-like area where you can walk and jog. Tram no. 4 goes daily from the Central Railway Station to Katajanokka every 5–10 minutes.
